summary: Tested Next.js 14 migration Phase 2 - Authentication and Dashboard functionality. Fixed critical middleware issue causing 404 on auth routes. All requested features verified working.

ui_bugs.issue: Logo image shows broken image icon - external image URL may have loading issues

passed_tests: Login page renders correctly at /login with all form elements
Register page renders correctly at /register with account type selection
My Account page renders correctly at /my-account with 4 service cards (employer, candidate, student, immigration)
Portal pages are protected and redirect to /login with redirect parameter
Admin dashboard is protected and redirects to /login when not authenticated
Homepage renders with navbar, hero section, stats, industries section, and footer
Language selector works in navbar - shows 8 languages dropdown
Industries page renders with all 5 industries (Construction, HoReCa, Agriculture, Manufacturing, Logistics)
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed middleware.ts - auth routes were being processed by next-intl middleware causing 404 errors
Added bypassIntlRoutes array to skip intl middleware for /login, /register, /my-account, /portal, /admin, /auth routes
All data-testid attributes properly implemented for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Next.js 14 migration Phase 2 complete. Middleware fix applied to bypass intl middleware for auth/dashboard routes. The Next.js app needs to be started on port 3001 for testing (port 3000 is used by old React app). Use: cd /app/frontend-next && npx next dev -p 3001

middleware_fix_applied.issue: next-intl middleware was intercepting auth routes (/login, /register, etc.) and returning 404
middleware_fix_applied.solution: Added bypassIntlRoutes array to skip intl middleware for auth and dashboard routes
